goal: Implement the smallest usable permission approval loop for the legacy web_Agent plugin so an out-of-whitelist write can produce a structured permission marker, be approved locally, and be retried without manual PowerShell copy/paste.
non_goals: Do not implement or fix the rewrite plugin roundtable workflow in F:\web_agents-new-plugin-rewrite.
Do not approve whole drives, home directories, or arbitrary roots by default.
Do not rewrite the bundled legacy plugin into React or TypeScript.
Do not make browser UI the final permission authority.
Do not edit content/index.iife.js unless the small helper script approach is proven insufficient.
acceptance_criteria: Out-of-whitelist write tools return a human-readable message plus a machine-readable web_Agent permission marker.
The marker includes requestId, operation/toolName, targetPaths, directoriesToApprove, argsHash, expiresAt, and suggestedApprovalRoot.
A local permission store can create pending requests, approve/reject them, issue a one-time approval token, and consume the token only once for matching operation, target paths, and argsHash.
The 3017 gateway exposes permission approve/reject routes without breaking /save-gpt-image and /save-tool-result.
The content result enhancer can parse a permission marker from tool output and expose approval UI metadata for browser rendering.
The legacy background script stores original mcp:call-tool payloads for permission requests and can approve then retry the original call once.
Automated tests cover the marker, store, gateway API behavior, and result enhancer parsing.
Static verification passes for modified scripts and extension files.
notes: User requested goal mode plus ADworkflo management and a report after completing this phase.
Claude qwen3.7-max technical review said implementation can start only for the old plugin permission-loop minimal slice.
TDD is required: write failing tests before production changes.
